Without a reporting mechanism, script integrity violations will not be detected or logged. NIST 800-172 3.14.1e requires the ability to detect and respond to software tampering events.

findings.details: No dependency manifest files were accessible at common web paths. Supply chain reconnaissance via file exposure is mitigated.

findings.evidence: CDN patterns checked across 3 pages, none found.
findings.details: No references to public CDN/package registries detected. Dependency confusion via CDN appears to be mitigated.

findings.evidence: All 4 responding endpoints report the same Server header: 'nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)'. Endpoint detail: [{"path": "/", "status": 200, "server": "nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)"}, {"path": "/admin/", "status": 200, "server": "nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)"}, {"path": "/api/ai_chat.php", "status": 404, "server": "nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)"}, {"path": "/admin/login.php", "status": 404, "server": "nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)"}]
findings.details: The web tier is homogeneous — every probed endpoint advertises the same server software. A single exploit targeting this server software would affect all endpoints equally. NIST SP 800-172 3.13.1e recommends architectural diversity to limit common-mode vulnerability propagation. Consider introducing a reverse proxy or CDN layer with a different technology to reduce monoculture risk.

findings.evidence: Technology stack fingerprint reveals a single-technology environment. Inventory: {"cms": ["WordPress"], "file_extensions": ["php"]}. Endpoint detail: [{"path": "/", "status": 200, "tech_indicators": ["Body: WordPress indicators"]}, {"path": "/admin/", "status": 200, "tech_indicators": ["Body: WordPress indicators"]}, {"path": "/api/ai_chat.php", "status": 404, "tech_indicators": ["Path extension: .php"]}, {"path": "/admin/login.php", "status": 404, "tech_indicators": ["Path extension: .php"]}]
findings.details: The detected technology stack is homogeneous — a single primary language/framework is present throughout. A single vulnerability in this technology (e.g., a PHP RCE, a framework deserialization flaw) would affect the entire application surface uniformly. NIST SP 800-172 3.13.1e recommends architectural diversity: consider introducing diverse components (e.g., a Go or Python microservice, a different CMS tier) to reduce monoculture risk and limit malicious-code propagation.

findings.evidence: Security header posture is inconsistent across endpoints. 3 endpoint(s) have a weaker posture than the strongest (2 headers): ['/', '/api/ai_chat.php', '/admin/login.php']. Posture gap: 2 missing header(s) on weaker endpoints. Detail: [{"path": "/", "status": 200, "security_headers": {"x-frame-options": "SAMEORIGIN"}}, {"path": "/admin/", "status": 200, "security_headers": {"x-frame-options": "SAMEORIGIN", "content-security-policy": "frame-ancestors 'self';"}}, {"path": "/api/ai_chat.php", "status": 404, "security_headers": {}}, {"path": "/admin/login.php", "status": 404, "security_headers": {}}]
findings.details: Different endpoints present different security header postures, indicating they may be served by distinct components with inconsistent hardening. While diversity itself is positive, inconsistent security policies suggest some components are inadequately hardened. Weaker endpoints may represent legacy or less-maintained technology components. NIST SP 800-172 3.13.1e — ensure all diverse components meet a consistent baseline security posture to avoid introducing weak links through architectural heterogeneity.

findings.evidence: No CDN or load-balancer headers detected across any probed endpoint. CDN headers checked: ['x-cdn', 'via', 'x-cache', 'cf-ray', 'x-amz-cf-id', 'x-varnish', 'x-cache-hits', 'x-cache-status', 'x-served-by', 'x-proxy-cache']. LB headers checked: ['x-request-id', 'x-upstream', 'x-forwarded-server', 'x-envoy-upstream-service-time']. Detail: [{"path": "/", "status": 200, "cdn_headers": [], "lb_headers": []}, {"path": "/admin/", "status": 200, "cdn_headers": [], "lb_headers": []}, {"path": "/api/ai_chat.php", "status": 404, "cdn_headers": [], "lb_headers": []}, {"path": "/admin/login.php", "status": 404, "cdn_headers": [], "lb_headers": []}]
findings.details: No CDN or load-balancing infrastructure was detected from HTTP response headers. The system appears to be served from a single origin without traffic distribution or CDN diversity. This represents a single point of failure and eliminates the geographic and infrastructure diversity that CDN/LB layers provide. NIST SP 800-172 3.13.1e — consider introducing CDN or load balancing to distribute risk and reduce the impact of single-node compromises or availability attacks.

findings.evidence: All 3 error probes returned identical format: 'plaintext'. Detail: [{"path": "/api/ai_chat.php", "method": "POST", "status": 404, "error_format": "plaintext"}, {"path": "/admin/login.php", "method": "POST", "status": 404, "error_format": "plaintext"}, {"path": "/api/nonexistent_endpoint_probe.php", "method": "GET", "status": 404, "error_format": "plaintext"}]
findings.details: All probed endpoints return errors in an identical format, suggesting a single centralised error handler or framework is in use across the entire application. While consistent error handling reduces information leakage, it is also a monoculture indicator — a single exploit that bypasses this error handler would affect every endpoint uniformly. NIST SP 800-172 3.13.1e — uniform error handling suggests component monoculture; consider segmenting error handling across independently implemented service tiers.

findings.evidence: No uptime disclosure found on status endpoints. Date header observed: 2026-03-08T03:43:22+00:00. Cannot determine server uptime from available headers.
findings.details: No explicit uptime information was disclosed. The Date header confirms the server is responding but does not reveal uptime. Manual verification of system refresh cadence is recommended to confirm NIST SP 800-172 3.14.4e compliance.

findings.evidence: Session cookie lifetime is 0 hours (1800s), within the acceptable threshold. Set-Cookie: cxq_uxlog_session=449d09a8-c797-481a-a157-95154573c601; expires=Sun, 08 Mar 2026 04:13:25 GMT; Max-Age=1800; path=/; HttpOnly
findings.details: Session expiry is set to 0 hours, which is within the 24-hour threshold. Short-lived sessions reduce the APT persistence window in line with NIST SP 800-172 3.14.4e intent.

findings.evidence: No static resources returned Last-Modified headers. Staleness could not be assessed from available responses.
findings.details: None of the probed static resources returned Last-Modified headers, so content freshness cannot be determined via HTTP. Manual verification of system refresh cadence is recommended.

findings.evidence: Server banner 'nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)' does not match any known outdated version signatures.
findings.details: Detected server components do not match known outdated version patterns. The software appears to be on a current major release, consistent with periodic system refresh per NIST SP 800-172 3.14.4e. Note: version suppression would provide stronger assurance by not disclosing version information at all.

findings.evidence: Dynamic endpoints returned no Cache-Control headers. Cache behavior is undefined — intermediate caches may apply their own heuristic TTLs.
findings.details: The absence of explicit Cache-Control headers on dynamic endpoints means that intermediate proxies and CDNs may cache responses using heuristic TTLs. This could allow stale or compromised data to persist in caches even after a system refresh. Explicit no-store or no-cache directives are recommended on dynamic API endpoints.
